title: Convert two-call enqueue to a transactional Ecto.Multi
prompt: The context in `test_fixtures/two_call_enqueue_context.ex` makes two separate calls: `Repo.insert(user)` followed by `Oban.insert(WelcomeEmailWorker.new(...))`. A crash between the two leaves a user with no welcome email. Rewrite `MyApp.Accounts.register_user/1` so both operations run inside a single `Ecto.Multi` transaction: insert the user, then call `Oban.insert(:welcome_email, fn %{user: user} -> WelcomeEmailWorker.new(%{"user_id" => user.id}) end)`. Return `{:ok, user}` or `{:error, changeset}`. Use `Repo.transaction/1` (not `Oban.transaction/1`). Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding Oban.insert's Multi form with a function that receives the Multi context — a step up from the easy tier's shape-only refactor.
research_citation: Oban module HexDocs — 'Ecto.Multi.new() |> Oban.insert("job-1", MyApp.Worker.new(%{id: 1})) |> Oban.insert("job-2", fn _ -> MyApp.Worker.new(%{id: 2}) end) |> MyApp.Repo.transaction()'
